chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# IVD Recipe: Workflow Orchestration Pattern
# ==============================================================================
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-01-24
# Category: workflow_patterns
# ==============================================================================

recipe:
  name: "Workflow Orchestration Pattern"
  version: "1.0"
  category: "workflow_patterns"
  created: "2026-01-24"
  author: "Leo Celis"
  
  description: |
    Standard pattern for documenting complex multi-step workflows that span multiple
    modules, functions, or services. This pattern captures the end-to-end process flow,
    making it easy for AI agents and developers to understand the complete workflow
    without reading all documentation or all code.
    
    Key characteristics:
    - Documents sequence of operations across multiple components
    - Maps each step to specific files and functions
    - Captures business logic at each step
    - Defines error handling and fallback strategies
    - Shows data transformations through the pipeline
    - Provides AI-friendly quick reference
    
    Use this pattern when:
    - Workflow spans 3+ distinct steps/modules
    - Multiple files/functions collaborate to complete a process
    - Business logic flows through several transformations
    - AI agents need quick workflow understanding
    - Onboarding requires workflow comprehension
  
  applies_to:
    - "Multi-step business processes (order processing, lead qualification, etc.)"
    - "Data pipelines with multiple transformation stages"
    - "Agent coordination workflows (multiple agents collaborating)"
    - "ETL workflows (extract, transform, load)"
    - "Content generation workflows (research → draft → review → publish)"
    - "API orchestration (calling multiple services in sequence)"
  
  when_to_use:
    - "Process has 3+ distinct steps that span multiple files/modules"
    - "Understanding requires reading multiple module docs or code files"
    - "AI agents frequently ask 'how does this workflow work?'"
    - "New developers take hours to understand the full process"
    - "Process involves multiple services/agents/functions"
    - "Business stakeholders need to understand technical flow"
  
  when_NOT_to_use:
    - "Simple 1-2 step processes (use module-level or task-level intent)"
    - "Single function with no external dependencies"
    - "Process is already self-explanatory from code"
    - "Workflow is trivial (< 3 steps with obvious flow)"

  placement:
    recommended: "workflows/{workflow_name}_intent.yaml (discoverability)"
    alternative: "{coordinator_dir}/{workflow_name}_intent.yaml when workflow is tightly coupled to a single orchestrator and not referenced independently (co-location with coordinator)"
    reference: "framework.md Workflow-Level Intents"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# TEMPLATE SECTIONS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

intent_template:
  summary: "[Domain] workflow for [purpose]"
  goal: "Process [input] through [N steps] to produce [ou

flow)"

  placement:
    recommended: "workflows/{workflow_name}_intent.yaml (discoverability)"
    alternative: "{coordinator_dir}/{workflow_name}_intent.yaml when workflow is tightly coupled to a single orchestrator and not referenced independently (co-location with coordinator)"
    reference: "framework.md Workflow-Level Intents"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# TEMPLATE SECTIONS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

intent_template:
  summary: "[Domain] workflow for [purpose]"
  goal: "Process [input] through [N steps] to produce [output] for [stakeholder]"
  success_metric: |
    - Workflow completion rate >= [95-99]%
    - End-to-end latency p95 < [target]
    - AI agent can understand workflow from intent file alone
  stakeholders:
    - "[Business stakeholder who owns the process]"
    - "[Technical team maintaining the workflow]"
    - "[End users or systems consuming workflow output]"

workflow_template:
  summary: "End-to-end [domain] process from [start] to [finish]"
  trigger: "[What initiates: API call | scheduled job | event | manual]"
  
  steps:
    - step: 1
      name: "[Verb] [noun] (e.g., Validate lead data)"
      file: "path/to/module.py"
      function: "function_name()"
      class: "ClassName"  # Optional
      description: |
        What this step does in business terms:
        - Input validation
        - Data normalization
        - Initial checks
      input: "What data enters this step (type and source)"
      output: "What data exits this step (type and shape)"
      business_logic: |
        Key rules and decisions:
        - Required fields must be present
        - Email format validation
        - Duplicate detection
      performance: "p95 < 100ms"  # Optional
      
    - step: 2
      name: "[Next action]"
      file: "path/to/another_module.py"
      function: "enrich_data()"
      description: "Enrich data with external sources"
      input: "Validated data from step 1"
      output: "Enriched data with additional fields"
      business_logic: |
        - Call external APIs (Clearbit, LinkedIn, etc.)
        - Merge external data with existing data
        - Handle missing data gracefully
      depends_on: [1]  # Explicit dependency
      performance: "p95 < 500ms"
      
    - step: 3
      name: "[Next action]"
      file: "path/to/agent.py"
      function: "process_with_agent()"
      description: "AI agent processes and classifies"
      input: "Enriched data from step 2"
      output: "Classification result with confidence score"
      business_logic: |
        - ML model inference
        - Threshold-based decision (>= 0.70 = qualified)
        - Confidence scoring
      depends_on: [2]
      performance: "p95 < 2s"
      
    - step: 4
      name: "[Final action]"
      file: "path/to/integration.py"
      function: "sync_to_external_system()"
      description: "Sync results to external system"
      input: "Classification from step 3"
      output: "External system record ID"
      business_logic: |
        - Map to external schema
        - Create or update record
        - Handle API rate limits

output: "Classification result with confidence score"
      business_logic: |
        - ML model inference
        - Threshold-based decision (>= 0.70 = qualified)
        - Confidence scoring
      depends_on: [2]
      performance: "p95 < 2s"
      
    - step: 4
      name: "[Final action]"
      file: "path/to/integration.py"
      function: "sync_to_external_system()"
      description: "Sync results to external system"
      input: "Classification from step 3"
      output: "External system record ID"
      business_logic: |
        - Map to external schema
        - Create or update record
        - Handle API rate limits
      depends_on: [3]
      performance: "p95 < 1s"
  
  data_flow:
    - "Raw input → Step 1 (validation) → Validated data"
    - "Validated data → Step 2 (enrichment) → Enriched data"
    - "Enriched data → Step 3 (classification) → Classification result"
    - "Classification → Step 4 (sync) → External record"
    - "Overall: Input → Validated → Enriched → Classified → Synced"
  
  error_handling:
    - step: 1
      failure_mode: "Invalid input data (missing fields, wrong format)"
      action: "Return error to caller with validation details"
      fallback: "None - fail fast on invalid input"
      
    - step: 2
      failure_mode: "External API unavailable or timeout"
      action: "Retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts)"
      fallback: "Use cached data or partial enrichment"
      
    - step: 3
      failure_mode: "AI model unavailable or inference error"
      action: "Log error and attempt fallback model"
      fallback: "Use rule-based classification"
      
    - step: 4
      failure_mode: "External system API error"
      action: "Queue for retry (background job)"
      fallback: "Store locally and retry later"
  
  orchestration:
    type: "sequential"  # or "parallel" or "mixed"
    coordinator: "path/to/coordinator.py or orchestrator.py"
    description: |
      Orchestration strategy:
      - Sequential: Steps 1-4 run in order (each waits for previous)
      - Parallel: Steps that can run simultaneously (e.g., multiple enrichment APIs)
      - Mixed: Some sequential (data dependencies), some parallel (independent)
      
      Coordinator responsibilities:
      - Manage step execution order
      - Handle errors and retries
      - Track workflow state
      - Emit events for monitoring
  
  performance:
    end_to_end_latency: "p95 < [target based on SLA]"
    typical_values: "Simple workflows: < 1s, Complex workflows: < 10s"
    throughput: ">= [N] workflows/minute"
    bottleneck_steps: ["List steps that are slowest or most resource-intensive"]
  
  monitoring:
    - metric: "workflow_completion_rate"
      target: ">= 95%"
      dashboard: "dashboards/[workflow_name]_dashboard.json"
      alert: "< 90% completion rate"
      
    - metric: "workflow_duration"
      target: "p95 < [target]"
      dashboard: "dashboards/[workflow_name]_dashboard.json"
      alert_threshold: "p95 > [critical_threshold]"
      
    - metric: "step_failure_rate"
      target: "< 5% per step"
      dashboard: "dashboards/[workflow_name]_dashboard.json"
      alert: "> 10% failure at any step"

constraints_t

minute"
    bottleneck_steps: ["List steps that are slowest or most resource-intensive"]
  
  monitoring:
    - metric: "workflow_completion_rate"
      target: ">= 95%"
      dashboard: "dashboards/[workflow_name]_dashboard.json"
      alert: "< 90% completion rate"
      
    - metric: "workflow_duration"
      target: "p95 < [target]"
      dashboard: "dashboards/[workflow_name]_dashboard.json"
      alert_threshold: "p95 > [critical_threshold]"
      
    - metric: "step_failure_rate"
      target: "< 5% per step"
      dashboard: "dashboards/[workflow_name]_dashboard.json"
      alert: "> 10% failure at any step"

constraints_template:
  - name: "workflow_completion_rate"
    requirement: "successful completion >= [95-99]%"
    typical_values: "95% minimum, 98% good, 99%+ excellent"
    test: "tests/test_[workflow_name]_integration.py::test_end_to_end"
    consequence_if_violated: "[Business impact - e.g., lost revenue, poor UX]"
    
  - name: "end_to_end_latency"
    requirement: "p95 latency < [target based on SLA]"
    typical_values: "Sync workflows: < 1s, Async workflows: < 10s"
    test: "tests/test_[workflow_name]_performance.py::test_latency"
    consequence_if_violated: "User experience degraded, SLA breach"
    
  - name: "step_idempotency"
    requirement: "each step can be retried safely without side effects"
    typical_values: "All steps must be idempotent"
    test: "tests/test_[workflow_name]_reliability.py::test_step_idempotency"
    consequence_if_violated: "Duplicate operations, data corruption"
    
  - name: "error_resilience"
    requirement: "workflow handles all documented failure modes gracefully"
    typical_values: "Graceful degradation, no silent failures"
    test: "tests/test_[workflow_name]_error_handling.py::test_all_failure_modes"
    consequence_if_violated: "Silent failures, data loss, stuck workflows"

rationale_template:
  decision: "Implement [workflow name] with [N-step] orchestrated process"
  reasoning: |
    Why this workflow design:
    - Step sequencing: [Why this order? What dependencies?]
    - Technology choices: [Why these tools/frameworks?]
    - Error handling strategy: [Why these fallbacks?]
    - Performance targets: [Why these SLAs?]
    
    Trade-offs accepted:
    - Sequential vs parallel: [Decision and rationale]
    - Synchronous vs asynchronous: [Decision and rationale]
    - Complexity vs simplicity: [Where we added complexity and why]
    
    Business context:
    - Who requested this workflow
    - What problem it solves
    - What alternatives were considered
  evidence: "playground/[workflow_name]_design_[date].md or .ipynb"
  stakeholder: "[Role] ([Name])"
  date: "[YYYY-MM-DD]"

alternatives_template:
  - name: "monolithic_approach"
    description: "Single function handles entire workflow"
    rejected_because: |
      - Harder to maintain (all logic in one place)
      - Harder to test (can't test steps independently)
      - Harder to scale (can't parallelize steps)
      - Harder to monitor (can't see where failures occur)
    when_it_works: "Very simple 2-step workflows with no error handling needs"
    
  - name: "event_driven_approach"
    description: "Each step

re considered
  evidence: "playground/[workflow_name]_design_[date].md or .ipynb"
  stakeholder: "[Role] ([Name])"
  date: "[YYYY-MM-DD]"

alternatives_template:
  - name: "monolithic_approach"
    description: "Single function handles entire workflow"
    rejected_because: |
      - Harder to maintain (all logic in one place)
      - Harder to test (can't test steps independently)
      - Harder to scale (can't parallelize steps)
      - Harder to monitor (can't see where failures occur)
    when_it_works: "Very simple 2-step workflows with no error handling needs"
    
  - name: "event_driven_approach"
    description: "Each step emits event, next step consumes event"
    rejected_because: |
      - Added complexity (event bus infrastructure)
      - Harder to debug (distributed tracing needed)
      - Overkill for synchronous workflows
    when_it_works: |
      - High-throughput async workflows
      - Workflows with fan-out/fan-in patterns
      - Microservices architecture
    
  - name: "workflow_engine"
    description: "Use Airflow, Temporal, or similar"
    rejected_because: |
      - Infrastructure overhead not justified
      - Workflow simple enough for code-based orchestration
      - Team not familiar with workflow engines
    when_it_works: |
      - Complex workflows with many branches
      - Workflows with human approval steps
      - Workflows requiring visual debugging

risks_template:
  - condition: "Step [N] becomes bottleneck (takes > [threshold])"
    impact: "Entire workflow slows down"
    action: "Add caching, optimize query, or parallelize"
    monitor: "dashboards/[workflow_name]_performance.json (step [N] duration)"
    severity: "high"
    
  - condition: "External dependency unavailable (step [N])"
    impact: "Workflow fails or uses degraded fallback"
    action: "Implement circuit breaker, use cached data"
    monitor: "logs/[workflow_name]_errors.log (external API failures)"
    severity: "high"
    
  - condition: "Workflow stuck in progress (not completing)"
    impact: "Resources leaked, monitoring confused"
    action: "Implement timeout and cleanup"
    monitor: "dashboards/[workflow_name]_stuck.json"
    severity: "medium"

implementation_template:
  current: "path/to/workflow_coordinator.py or orchestrator.py"
  entry_point: "function or endpoint that triggers workflow"
  steps_mapping:
    - step: 1
      file: "path/to/module1.py"
      function: "function1()"
    - step: 2
      file: "path/to/module2.py"
      function: "function2()"
  version: 1
  tests: "tests/test_[workflow_name]_*.py"
  documentation: "docs/workflows/[workflow_name].md"

verification_template:
  integration_tests:
    - name: "end_to_end_happy_path"
      description: "Complete workflow executes successfully"
      input: "Valid input matching production scenarios"
      expected_output: "Successful completion with expected final state"
      validates_constraint: "workflow_completion_rate"
      
    - name: "step_N_failure_recovery"
      description: "Workflow recovers from step N failure"
      input: "Valid input"
      mock: "Step N fails with [error]"
      expected_output: "Workflow uses fallback and completes"

    - name: "step_N_failure_recovery"

  monitoring_verification:

      description: "Dashboard shows all workflow metrics"
      verification: "Manual check of dashboard completeness"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# REAL-WORLD EXAMPLES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

examples:
  - name: "Lead Qualification Workflow"
    description: |
      5-step workflow: Receive lead → Enrich data → Score lead → 
      Qualify decision → Sync to CRM
    
    files:
      - "api/endpoints/leads.py (step 1: receive)"
      - "jobs/enrich_lead_job.py (step 2: enrich)"
      - "agent/lead_qualifier/scoring.py (step 3: score)"
      - "agent/lead_qualifier/agent.py (step 4: qualify)"
      - "integrations/salesforce/sync.py (step 5: sync)"
    
    key_characteristics:
      - Sequential execution (each step depends on previous)
      - External dependencies (Clearbit API, Salesforce API)
      - ML inference (scoring step)
      - Error handling (retry strategies, fallbacks)
      - Monitoring (completion rate, latency per step)
  
  - name: "Article Generation Workflow"
    description: |
      7-step workflow: Trigger → Plan → Research → Generate → 
      Fact-check → Generate images → Publish
    
    files:
      - "api/slack/commands.py (step 1: trigger)"
      - "agent/creative/agent.py (steps 2, 4: plan, generate)"
      - "libs/google/search_intelligence.py (step 3: research)"
      - "agent/fact_checker/utils.py (step 5: fact-check)"
      - "job/creative/generate_article_images_job.py (step 6: images)"
      - "integrations/wordpress/publish.py (step 7: publish)"
    
    key_characteristics:
      - Mixed orchestration (mostly sequential, images in background)
      - Multiple AI agents (creative agent, fact checker)
      - Long-running steps (image generation takes 5-10 minutes)
      - Quality checks (fact-checking before publish)
  
  - name: "ETL Data Pipeline Workflow"
    description: |
      4-step workflow: Extract → Transform → Validate → Load
    
    files:
      - "jobs/extract_data_job.py (step 1: extract)"
      - "jobs/transform_data_job.py (step 2: transform)"
      - "jobs/validate_data_job.py (step 3: validate)"
      - "jobs/load_data_job.p

/publish.py (step 7: publish)"
    
    key_characteristics:
      - Mixed orchestration (mostly sequential, images in background)
      - Multiple AI agents (creative agent, fact checker)
      - Long-running steps (image generation takes 5-10 minutes)
      - Quality checks (fact-checking before publish)
  
  - name: "ETL Data Pipeline Workflow"
    description: |
      4-step workflow: Extract → Transform → Validate → Load
    
    files:
      - "jobs/extract_data_job.py (step 1: extract)"
      - "jobs/transform_data_job.py (step 2: transform)"
      - "jobs/validate_data_job.py (step 3: validate)"
      - "jobs/load_data_job.py (step 4: load)"
    
    key_characteristics:
      - Batch processing (large datasets)
      - Data validation between steps
      - Rollback capability (if validation fails)
      - Scheduled execution (daily or hourly)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# USAGE GUIDE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

usage_guide:
  when_to_create_workflow_intent: |
    Create a workflow-level intent when:
    1. Process spans 3+ files or modules
    2. AI agents ask "how does this work end-to-end?"
    3. Onboarding requires understanding the full flow
    4. Business stakeholders need technical workflow understanding
    5. Workflow has multiple error paths or fallbacks
    
    DON'T create workflow intent for:
    1. Simple 1-2 step processes (use module-level)
    2. Single-function operations (use task-level)
    3. Self-explanatory code flows
  
  how_to_use_this_recipe: |
    Step 1: Copy intent.yaml template
    Step 2: Set scope.level = "workflow"
    Step 3: Fill in workflow section with steps
    Step 4: Map each step to file/function
    Step 5: Document data flow and error handling
    Step 6: Add constraints and monitoring
    
    Result: Single YAML file that explains entire workflow
  
  ai_agent_benefits: |
    With workflow intent, AI agents can:
    - Understand end-to-end process from one file
    - Know which files/functions to examine for specific steps
    - See error handling without reading all code
    - Get business context for technical decisions
    - Quickly answer "how does X workflow work?"
    
    Cost reduction:
    - Before: Read 5-10 files to understand workflow
    - After: Read 1 workflow intent file
    - Savings: 80-90% reduction in context needed

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# REFERENCES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

references:
  - name: "IVD Framework"
    link: "../framework.md"
    description: "Core IVD principles and intent artifacts"
  
  - name: "Intent Levels Guide"
    link: "../templates/intent_levels_guide.md"
    description: "When to use system/workflow/module/task levels"
  
  - name: "Agent Classifier Recipe"
    link: "./agent-classifier.yaml"
    description: "Pattern for AI classification agents (often part of workflows)"
  
  - name: "Background Job Recipe"
    link: "./infra-background-job.yaml"
    description: "Pattern for async jobs (often workflow steps)"
